# Figure description: Схема_изоглубин_по_уровню_T4.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Схема_изоглубин_по_уровню_T4.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **isoline (isodepth) map** — specifically a **схема изоглубин** (depth contour scheme/map) — constructed on a reference seismic reflector horizon designated **T₄** (Т₊ in the original notation). It is a structural-seismic interpretation map compiled at a scale of **1:500,000**, showing the depth to a key subsurface reflecting horizon across a regional survey area in northern Kazakhstan.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a multi-sheet composite area in **northern Kazakhstan**, assembled from individual map sheets arranged in a grid pattern visible in the inset **"Схема расположения листов"** (sheet layout diagram). The sheet grid references visible in the inset include: **XXXII, XXXIV, LXV** (top row), **I, II, V** (middle rows), **VII, VIII, IX** (lower rows), and **XIV, XV** (bottom row). The survey was conducted by the **Центральная геолого-поисковая экспедиция** (Central Geological Prospecting Expedition) of the **Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение** (Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association), under the **Министерство геологии Каз.ССР** (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR).

The figure is dated **1964** (visible in the upper portion) and is referenced as **чертёж № 47** (drawing No. 47). A note in the lower right credits the source: **"Из отчёта Котлярба А.И. за 1964 г., ЦКПГО"** (From the report of Kotlyarba A.I. for 1964, CKPGO). There is also a reference in the upper right to the **Западно-Тенизская партия за 1918–64 гг.** (West-Teniz Party, 1918–1964), suggesting the eastern portion of the map relates to the **Teniz** area — likely near **Lake Tengiz** in the Karaganda/Akmola region of Kazakhstan. A stamp of the **Казахской ССР геологический фонд** (Geological Fund of the Kazakh SSR) with accession number **36572** is visible centrally, consistent with the report context.

---

## Key Content

### Isodepth Contours (Изоглубины):
- The map displays a dense network of **depth contour lines (isoglubiny/изоглубины)** to the T₄ reflector horizon, drawn as both solid and dashed lines.
- **Dashed contours** likely indicate interpolated or less certain values.
- The **depth intensity scale** ("Шкала интенсивности изоглубин в м") in the legend shows values ranging from approximately **600 m to beyond 1000+ m** (specific tick values appear to read approximately: **600, 800, 1000, более** [more than]).

### Structural Features and Patterns:
- **Uplift zones (anticlinal/dome structures)** are indicated by **closed contour highs** shown with **pink/salmon shading** — these are the most prospective structures for hydrocarbon or mineral accumulation. Several such closures are visible:
  - A prominent **pink-shaded dome/anticline** in the **west-central** portion of the map.
  - Additional smaller pink-shaded highs in the **southwest** and **left-center** portions.
  - These represent **relatively shallow depths** to the T₄ reflector, indicating structural highs.

- **Deep zones** are represented by **dark grey to black shading**, indicating areas of maximum depth to the reflector — structural lows or grabens/depressions. These are concentrated in the **north-central**, **northeast**, and portions of the **northwest**.

- **Cross-hatched zones** (diagonal hatching pattern) in the northeast and upper portions represent either:
  - **Зоны потери корреляции** (zones of loss of correlation) — areas where the seismic reflector could not be reliably traced, associated with faulting or disruption of layers, **OR**
  - Outcrops of specific formations (see legend below).

### Legend (Условные обозначения) — Key:
The legend identifies the following map symbols:
1. **Изоглубины** — Depth contour lines (isodepth lines).
2. **Зоны потери корреляции ОГ, предположительно связанные с разрывными нарушениями или крутым залеганием слоёв** — Zones of loss of correlation of the reflecting horizon, presumably associated with faults or steep dip of strata (shown as cross-hatched zones).
3. **Разрывные нарушения предполагаемые по сейсморазведочным данным** — Inferred faults based on seismic survey data (shown as diagonal line-hatched zones).
4. **Выходы на поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты в бане древних пород по геологическим данным** — Outcrops of the **Vladimirskaya Svita** (Владимировская свита) deposits within the basement of ancient rocks, based on geological data (shown as dark stippled pattern).
5. **Выходы на поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты в зоне древних пород по геосейсмическим данным** — Outcrops of Vladimirskaya Svita deposits in the zone of ancient rocks based on **geoseismic data** (shown as a lighter stippled/dotted pattern).
6. **Границы Тенизской впадины** — **Boundary of the Teniz Depression** (Тенизская впадина) — shown as a bold line, defining the structural basin associated with Lake Tengiz.
